title: fagraea woodiana
intro: Fagraea woodiana, also known as the Frangipani tree or Temple tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and soothing skin irritations. Fagraea woodiana can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
